verb_suffix.label: Verb stem suffixes
verb_suffix.description: Attaches to verb stem: Vます-stem + _

confusionGroups.label: Degree/Limit Particles
confusionGroups.description: Particles expressing degree, limitation, emphasis, or exclusion after nouns/phrases
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: Students confuse which particle expresses 'only/even/just/to the extent of' in context. さえ emphasizes an extreme case; しか requires negative verb; こそ intensifies; くらい/ぐらい indicates approximate degree; ばかり indicates 'just did' or 'nothing but'

confusionGroups.label: Hearsay & Conjecture
confusionGroups.description: Expressions conveying reported information or probabilistic inference
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: そう(hearsay) = I heard; らしい = apparently/evidently; よう = seems like (speaker inference); みたい = looks like (casual); だろう = probably; であろう = formal probably; の様 = seems like (formal/written). Key distinction: information source and formality level

confusionGroups.label: Appearance & Tendency
confusionGroups.description: Expressions describing how something appears or a habitual tendency
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: っぽい = -ish, characteristic feel; がち = tend to (often negative); やすい = easy to do; にくい = hard to do. All attach to verb stems or nouns but convey different nuances of tendency

confusionGroups.label: Te-form Auxiliaries
confusionGroups.description: Auxiliary verbs following the te-form expressing aspect, direction, or social relationship
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: Critical to distinguish: ている (ongoing/state), てしまう (completion/regret), てある (resultant state purposely made), てみる (try), てくる (come back after doing / gradual change), てあげる/てくれる/てもらう (giving/receiving of actions with directionality)

confusionGroups.label: Purpose & Intent
confusionGroups.description: Expressions conveying purpose, intent, or goal-directed action
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: ために = concrete purpose or cause; ように = goal state (used with intransitive/potential); ことにする = decide to do; ことになる = it has been decided (external); ようになる = change to be able to; 意向形と思う = I think I will; 予定 = planned; 積もりだった = had intended but didn't

confusionGroups.label: Cause & Reason
confusionGroups.description: Expressions linking cause to effect or explaining reasons
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: おかげで = thanks to (positive cause); せいで = because of (negative cause/blame); ことから = reason based on observable fact; ものだから = unavoidable/justifying excuse; なぜなら = because (formal, front-loaded); それで = and so, as a result; し = and also (multiple reasons stacked)

confusionGroups.label: Temporal Expressions
confusionGroups.description: Expressing when, during, while, or simultaneous events
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: 時 = when (general); うちに = while (before change); ところ = just at the moment of; まま = as it remains; ついでに = while already doing something else; じゅう/ちゅう = throughout (all of); までに = by (deadline); 時間がたつ = time passing

confusionGroups.label: Topic & Reference Particles
confusionGroups.description: Post-noun particles expressing various relationships: about, toward, for, as, against, through
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: について = about/regarding (topic); に対して = toward/in contrast; にとって = for (perspective of); として = as/in capacity of; に反して = against/contrary to; を中心に = centering on; によって(1249) = by/due to (agent/means); によって(976) = depending on (variation); を通して = through/by means of

confusionGroups.label: Contrast & Concession
confusionGroups.description: Expressing contrast, unexpected results, or concession
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: のに(contrast 1493) = but/although (emotional/surprising); のに(purpose 551) = in order to; のに(wish 1593) = I wish; 反面 = on the other hand; にしては = for/considering; としても = even if/assuming; は(660) = contrastive topic marker

confusionGroups.label: Obligation & Modal
confusionGroups.description: Expressions conveying obligation, prohibition, permission, expectation, or conjecture
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: べき = should (moral/rational expectation); なければならない = must; せねばならない = must (formal/literary); てはいけない = must not; はず = supposed to be/expected; 予定 = planned/scheduled; 積もりだった = had intended (but didn't)

confusionGroups.label: Verb Completion & Continuation
confusionGroups.description: Verb suffixes expressing completion, continuation, beginning, or mutual action
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: 切る = completely/to the end; 続ける = keep doing; 出す(1728) = suddenly begin; 出す(1662) = begin (same); 合う = do together/mutually; 直す = do again/redo; 出す(1626) = pay/issue/stick out (different meaning)

confusionGroups.label: Conditional Forms
confusionGroups.description: If/when/given constructions
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: なら = if (given the situation); と = when/if (automatic consequence, natural sequence); ば(条件形) = if (hypothetical, focus on condition); としても = even if (concessive conditional); せねばならない shares conditional ば form

confusionGroups.label: Comparison & Extent
confusionGroups.description: Expressing degree of comparison or extent to which something applies
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: ほど = to the extent that / not as~as~; より = than/rather than; に比べて = compared to; くらい/ぐらい = about/approximately/to the extent; 比べものにならない = no comparison possible

confusionGroups.label: Decision & Change of State
confusionGroups.description: Expressions about decisions made or changes in state/ability
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: ことにする(1629/1769) = decide to do (volitional); ことになる(1732) = it has been decided (external/natural); にする = decide on (choose from options); になる = become; ようになる = come to be able to (gradual change)

confusionGroups.label: Without / Negation
confusionGroups.description: Expressions meaning 'without doing' or negating necessity
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: ずに = without doing (literary/formal negative gerund); なし = without (noun modifier); なくてもすむ = don't need to / can get by without

confusionGroups.label: Manner & Similarity
confusionGroups.description: Expressing how something is done or that it resembles something else
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: みたい = looks like/seems (casual appearance); よう = seems/in the manner of; ように = in order to / so that; の様 = seems like (formal); のような = just like/similar to

confusionGroups.label: Question & Wonder Particles
confusionGroups.description: Sentence-final particles expressing questions, wonder, or uncertainty
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: の(question) = question (female/casual); かな = I wonder; だい = casual question (male); かしら = I wonder (female); かい = casual yes/no question (male); か = question; かどうか = whether or not

confusionGroups.label: Emphasis & Assertion Particles
confusionGroups.description: Particles that add emphasis, emotional color, or assertion
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: よ = assertion/new info; なあ/な = emotional emphasis/reflection; わ = mild emphasis (female); ってば = emotional insistence; って = quotation/assertion marker

confusionGroups.label: Assumption & Supposition
confusionGroups.description: Expressing assumptions, hypotheticals, or regarding something as X
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: とする = assume/regard as; として(try 1597) = try to (volitional+として); だろう = probably/I suppose; であろう = formal probably; からして = even/judging from; みたい = like/as if (assumption)

confusionGroups.label: Advisory, Permission & Prohibition
confusionGroups.description: Expressions giving advice, permission, or prohibition
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: な(prohibition) = don't do (command); なさい = do (informal imperative); 方がいい = it would be better to; べき = should; てはいけない = must not; てもいいですか = may I/do you mind if

confusionGroups.label: Polite Requests & Formulas
confusionGroups.description: Fixed polite expressions for requests, messages, and social formulas
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: くださいませんか = would you please (polite request); と伝えていただけませんか = please convey my message; どうぞ = please/go ahead; ということですね = so your point is

confusionGroups.label: Giving & Receiving Actions
confusionGroups.description: Verbs of giving and receiving, including beneficial actions
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: てあげる = do for someone (outward, speaker gives); てくれる = do for me (inward, speaker receives); てもらう = have/get someone do for me. Direction of benefit is the key distinction

confusionGroups.label: Location, Direction & Movement
confusionGroups.description: Particles and expressions marking location, direction, or path
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: で/に/前 = place/time prepositions; を(area) = area moved through; を(departure) = marks departure point; 〜中に/上に/下に = relative spatial location (inside/above/below)

confusionGroups.label: Informational Clauses & Nominalizers
confusionGroups.description: Expressions that nominalize or frame clauses as information units
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: わけ = meaning/reason/circumstances (logical conclusion); わけではない = doesn't necessarily mean; の(nominalizer) = turns verb phrase into noun; のだ = explanation/reason; か(embedded) = the question of whether

confusionGroups.label: Formal Connectors & Discourse Markers
confusionGroups.description: Words that connect ideas, add information, or organize discourse
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: その上 = moreover/besides; それに = and furthermore; ところで = by the way (topic shift); そうすると = if that's the case/then; さもないと = or else/otherwise

confusionGroups.label: Gradual Change & Progression
confusionGroups.description: Expressions indicating gradual change over time
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: 次第に = gradually/little by little; 今では = by now/nowadays (implies change from before); ようになる = come to do (gradual change to new state)

confusionGroups.label: Mental State & Emotion
confusionGroups.description: Expressions about inner mental or emotional states
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: 気がする = feel/have a feeling that; 気になる = be worried/concerned about; 儚い = fleeting/hopeless (adjective state); つい = unthinkingly/despite oneself

confusionGroups.label: Miscellaneous Adverbials
confusionGroups.description: Standalone adverbial expressions that don't fit other groups
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: どうせ = after all/anyway; できるだけ = as much as possible; 少しも~ない = not at all; なんとなく = vaguely/somehow; どうしても = absolutely/no matter what; どちらかといえば = if I had to say; せっかく = with effort/since we're here; あっと言う間 = in a flash; とっさに = reflexively; だからこそ = precisely because; とても~ない = can't possibly

confusionGroups.label: Limit & Frequency
confusionGroups.description: Expressions about limits, doing something to excess, or frequency
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: に限って = of all people/things (ironic limit); ~放題 = as much as one likes/unlimited; 挙げ句 = in the end after all that (negative outcome after prolonged effort)

confusionGroups.label: Miscellaneous Conjunctions
confusionGroups.description: Conjunctions and clause linkers that don't fit main groups
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: て(connect) = sequential/parallel verb connector; と共に = along with/at the same time; し = and/plus (multiple reasons); か何か = or something like that; というのに = although/despite; であろうが = whether A or B it doesn't matter

confusionGroups.label: Miscellaneous Reference Expressions
confusionGroups.description: Reference, equivalence, and labeling expressions
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: と言うと = speaking of/when you mention; によると = according to; とのこと = according to (reported speech); 旨 = that/concerning; と同様 = in the same way as; 並みに = to the same degree as

confusionGroups.label: Miscellaneous Grammar Forms
confusionGroups.description: Grammar forms that are syntactically isolated or highly specific
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: である = formal copula; なる(adj) = used like な of na-adjectives; な(particle) = na-adjective particle; ばかり = have just done; わざとらしい = fake/pretending; もとい = rather/correction; なぜなら = because (formal)

confusionGroups.label: Miscellaneous Sentence-Final
confusionGroups.description: Sentence-final expressions for emotion, memory, or modality
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: ~たっけ = was it~/did I recall correctly; ~なかったろう = probably didn't; よくも~ものだ = how dare/how could; のに(wish) = I wish; うちに = while (before it changes)

confusionGroups.label: Miscellaneous (Low Frequency / Edge Cases)
confusionGroups.description: Grammar points with unique roles or very low usage counts
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: Various low-frequency expressions

confusionGroups.label: Comparison & Similarity (Formal)
confusionGroups.description: Formal expressions for comparing or equating things
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: 比べものにならない = no comparison possible; と同様 = same way as; 並みに = to same degree as; とは限らない = not necessarily/not always true

confusionGroups.label: Passive & Causative
confusionGroups.description: Make/let/have someone do; passive expressions
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: させる = causative (make/let someone do). Related to passive forms but here focused on causative usage

confusionGroups.label: Frequency & Habit
confusionGroups.description: Expressions about habitual actions, repeated occurrences, or tendencies
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: がち = tend to (often negative habit); まま = remaining unchanged (habitual state); つい = reflexive habit despite intention

confusionGroups.label: First Time & Recurrence
confusionGroups.description: Expressions about doing something for the first time in a while or redoing
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: ~ぶり = first time in ~ (time span); ~直す = to do over/redo from scratch

confusionGroups.label: Topic & Discourse Markers (は, って)
confusionGroups.description: Topic marking and discourse-level particles
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: は(contrast) = contrastive topic; って = quotation/topic (casual); ってば = insistence on topic; と言うと = speaking of (topic introduction)

confusionGroups.label: Clause-Final Expressions
confusionGroups.description: Expressions that end subordinate clauses with special nuance
confusionGroups.pedagogicalNote: ものだから = because (unavoidable excuse); 反面 = on the other hand; ことから = from the fact that; おうとすると = just when trying to do (interrupted); ようとする = try to do (unsuccessful)

grammarPoints.meaning: center on, in the middle of
and then, after that; due to that
it does not necessarily mean that
to give me, to do for me
in order to, because of, for
in a flash, before one knows it
indicates a change which has taken place
I am about to / I was about to
something purposely put into a state
when you talk of, speaking of
to pay, place, issue, stick out
to the extent of; not as~as~; about
absolutely, by all means, no matter what
believe that it is so, supposed to be
as well as; with; at the same time

grammarPoints.point: ところに、ところへ、ところを

grammarPoints.meaning: I had the intention to do (but didn't)
that means, if that's the case

grammarPoints.point: と伝えていただけませんか

てはいけない.past_negative: てはいけなくなかった

synonymGroups.reason: から and ので are often interchangeable for 'because', but ので is softer and more objective; から is more direct and subjective
らしい, よう, みたい, そう(hearsay), の様 all express 'seems/apparently' but differ in formality, information source, and directness of inference
なければならない, せねばならない, なければなりません are near-synonyms for obligation, differing mainly in formality and register
ことにする (1629 and 1769 are the same pattern, different IDs — volitional decision) vs ことになる (external/natural result that has been decided)
しか (requires negative verb), さえ (even/only — extremity marker), だけ (just/only — neutral), ばかり (nothing but/just did), こそ (emphasis on this one thing)
